purpose: In-container bootstrap that runs as PID 1 via `python3 -m entrypoint` (baked into the Nix image at /lib/python/entrypoint, launched by the /bin/yolo-entrypoint shell wrapper). Reads the YOLO_* env contract set by the host CLI, then generates everything a jail session needs — PATH shims, agent launchers, per-agent MCP/LSP configs (claude/copilot/gemini/opencode/pi/codex), bashrc, mise config, CA bundle, timezone/ld.so.cache, helper scripts — spawns socat port-forwarders and the jail-daemon supervisor, and finally execs bash with the requested command. Also imported as a plain Python library by the host CLI (`yolo check` dry-run, macos-user bootstrap, agent_registry data).

files.role: main() orchestration; path constants from env (HOME/JAIL_HOME, NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX, GOPATH, MISE_DATA_DIR); perf log ~/.yolo-perf.log; user-env hydration (regex parse of yolo-user-env.sh); YOLO_LSP_SERVERS/YOLO_AGENTS loaders; cgroup-delegate socket check; nvim config copy; mise trust; exec_bash (final os.execvp)

Per-agent config writers: copilot/gemini/claude/opencode/pi/codex; shared MCP-server resolution (presets + YOLO_MCP_SERVERS merge, requires_env gating, ${VAR} interpolation); claude extras (3-way settings merge, host-file sync, history isolation, credentials symlink+harvest, plugin install); minimal TOML emitter for codex; CONFIG_WRITERS map
Pure-data registry (AgentSpec/InstallSpec/BriefingSpec) for 6 agents; DEFAULT_AGENTS=[claude]; ALL_MISE_RETIRE/ALL_OVERLAY_DIRS; resolve_agents(). Stdlib-only ON PURPOSE — imported by host CLI too
git config --global user.name/email/core.excludesFile and jj config set --user from YOLO_GIT_*/YOLO_JJ_* (best-effort subprocess)
Writes 3 static bash wrappers: chrome-devtools-mcp-wrapper (headless chromium autostart on :9222), mcp-wrappers/node, mcp-wrappers/npx (mise-shim bypass)

~/.config/mise/config.toml generation via regex surgery on existing TOML text: dedupe keys, retire agent tools (also edits /workspace/mise.toml!), 'system'→real version rewrite, YOLO_MISE_TOOLS injection; mise uninstall --all of retired tools

iptables DNAT for YOLO_PUBLISHED_PORTS; socat spawns for YOLO_FORWARD_HOST_PORTS (unix-socket vs TCP-gateway mode); jail-daemon supervisor singleton fork (python -m src.jail_daemon_supervisor, PID file /tmp/yolo-jail-supervisor.pid, kill(pid,0) liveness)

Emits standalone Python helper scripts as string literals: yolo-cglimit (unix-socket JSON client), yolo-journalctl (framed stream client), yolo-ps (src.yolo_ps wrapper), yolo shim (~/.yolo-shims/yolo + _yolo_bootstrap.py via uv run)

generate_bashrc (prompt, PATH, CA env, aliases, mise activate interactive-only); .yolo-bootstrap.sh (MCP/LSP npm+go install/uninstall w/ ~/.yolo-installed-lsps sentinel); .yolo-venv-precreate.sh (mise deadlock workaround, inline python3 tomllib parser)

~/.yolo-shims: blocked-tool shims from YOLO_BLOCK_CONFIG (case-glob argv filtering, exit 127, YOLO_BYPASS_SHIMS escape); lazy-update agent launchers (npm + native curl|bash kinds, 3600s stamp); pnpm lazy launcher

configure_timezone (/run/localtime symlink + /run/timezone from $TZ/$TZDIR); generate_ld_cache (ldconfig -C /run/ld.so.cache); generate_ca_bundle (~/.yolo-ca-bundle.crt from SSL_CERT_FILE baseline + NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS extras, exports 4 trust env vars)

public_surface.name: yolo-entrypoint [args...]
public_surface.detail: argv[1:] joined with spaces → command string (default 'bash'). Runs full setup then execs `bash --rcfile ~/.bashrc -c '<source yolo-user-env.sh>; eval "$(mise env -s bash)" 2>/dev/null; <cmd>'`. Prints '⚡ Executing: <cmd>' to stderr unless cmd=='bash' or contains 'yolo-bootstrap'. Set as image Cmd AND re-invoked on every `podman exec <cname> yolo-entrypoint <cmd>` (must be idempotent).
public_surface.consumers: flake.nix /bin/yolo-entrypoint wrapper (PYTHONPATH=/lib/python; exec python3 -m entrypoint); cli/run_cmd.py container create + all exec paths

public_surface.name: JAIL_HOME / HOME / NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX / GOPATH / MISE_DATA_DIR
public_surface.detail: Path roots read AT IMPORT TIME into module constants (HOME, SHIM_DIR, NPM_BIN, GO_BIN, MISE_SHIMS=$MISE_DATA_DIR/shims fallback ~/.local/share/mise/shims). MISE_DATA_DIR=/mise in jails, absent on host — import must not crash without it.
public_surface.consumers: cli sets JAIL_HOME=tmp for `yolo check`; macos_user sets before import

public_surface.name: YOLO_AGENTS
public_surface.detail: JSON list of agent names; unknown names dropped; unset/garbage → ["claude"]. Gates launcher generation, bashrc aliases, and which configure_* run.

public_surface.name: YOLO_BLOCK_CONFIG
public_surface.detail: JSON list of {name, message, suggestion, block_flags:[case-glob patterns]}. name in (grep,find) → passthrough shim to /bin/<name>; block_flags present → argv-filtered block (long-exact patterns first, then --* skip, then short patterns); absent → unconditional exit 127. YOLO_BYPASS_SHIMS=1 bypasses.

public_surface.name: YOLO_MCP_PRESETS / YOLO_MCP_SERVERS
public_surface.detail: PRESETS: JSON array from {chrome-devtools, sequential-thinking}. SERVERS: JSON object merged on top; null value removes; `requires_env:[VARS]` gates on non-empty env (key stripped before agents see it); env values get ${VAR} interpolation against startup env (undefined → literal + stderr warning).

public_surface.name: YOLO_LSP_SERVERS
public_surface.detail: JSON object name→{command,args,fileExtensions}; merged over empty defaults. Drives copilot lsp-config.json, gemini <name>-lsp MCP wrap via mcp-language-server, claude plugin install/uninstall + enabledPlugins + ENABLE_LSP_TOOL env.

public_surface.name: YOLO_MISE_TOOLS
public_surface.detail: JSON object tool→version merged over base {node:22, python:3.13, go:latest} into ~/.config/mise/config.toml (injected always override; base add-if-missing).

public_surface.name: YOLO_GIT_NAME / YOLO_GIT_EMAIL / YOLO_GLOBAL_GITIGNORE / YOLO_JJ_NAME / YOLO_JJ_EMAIL
public_surface.detail: Identity injection via `git config --global` / `jj config set --user`; GLOBAL_GITIGNORE is a path (must be existing file) → core.excludesFile.

public_surface.name: YOLO_HOST_DIR
public_surface.detail: Host workspace path; shown in PS1 banner; sha256[:12] keys claude per-jail history file.

public_surface.name: YOLO_HOST_CLAUDE_FILES
public_surface.detail: JSON list of filenames mounted at /ctx/host-claude/; all copied to ~/.claude/ except settings.json which gets the 3-way merge.

public_surface.name: YOLO_PUBLISHED_PORTS
public_surface.detail: JSON array of 'PORT/PROTO' → iptables -t nat PREROUTING DNAT to 127.0.0.1:PORT (needs route_localnet sysctl set by cli; silent skip w/o iptables).

public_surface.name: YOLO_FORWARD_HOST_PORTS / YOLO_FWD_HOST_GATEWAY
public_surface.detail: JSON array (int | 'p' | 'p:x'); per port spawn `socat TCP-LISTEN:p,bind=127.0.0.1,fork,reuseaddr <target>`; target = TCP:$GATEWAY:p when gateway set (macOS), else UNIX-CONNECT:/tmp/yolo-fwd/port-p.sock (host socat side in cli/network.py). Skips already-bound ports (exec idempotency). stderr → ~/.yolo-socat.log.

public_surface.name: YOLO_JAIL_DAEMONS
public_surface.detail: Non-empty → fork `sys.executable -m src.jail_daemon_supervisor` once (PYTHONPATH=$YOLO_REPO_ROOT, stdout/err DEVNULL, same process group); singleton via /tmp/yolo-jail-supervisor.pid + kill(pid,0).

public_surface.name: YOLO_REPO_ROOT
public_surface.detail: Default /opt/yolo-jail. Baked into generated yolo-ps, _yolo_bootstrap.py (yolo wrapper), and supervisor PYTHONPATH — the in-jail bridge to the Python src/ tree.

public_surface.name: TZ / TZDIR
public_surface.detail: → /run/localtime symlink to $TZDIR/$TZ + /run/timezone text (image symlinks /etc/{localtime,timezone} → /run/*; root fs is ro). No-op if TZ unset or zone file missing.
public_surface.consumers: Go time pkg, date, anything reading /etc/localtime

public_surface.name: SSL_CERT_FILE / NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS (in) → SSL_CERT_FILE, REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE, CURL_CA_BUNDLE, GIT_SSL_CAINFO (out)
public_surface.detail: generate_ca_bundle concatenates baseline + colon-separated loophole CAs into ~/.yolo-ca-bundle.crt (0644, always written even if empty), then rewrites the 4 vars in os.environ so every child (supervisor, socat, bash) inherits them; bashrc re-exports for interactive shells.
public_surface.consumers: all TLS clients in jail; cli assembles NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS

public_surface.name: ~/.config/yolo-user-env.sh hydration
public_surface.detail: Entrypoint parses `export KEY=${KEY:-'value'}` (plus bare/'sq'/"dq" forms, '\'' unescape) into os.environ, launch-env-wins precedence — MUST stay in sync with the writer in cli/run_cmd.py (~line 1991). Needed so MCP ${VAR} interpolation sees env_sources values before bash sources the file.
public_surface.consumers: written by cli, sourced by bashrc + exec_bash

public_surface.name: ~/.yolo-shims/ (first on PATH)
public_surface.detail: Blocked-tool shims; lazy agent launchers (npm kind: install/update via `npm install -g PKG@latest --prefer-online`, jq version probe, 3600s stamp in ~/.cache/yolo-agent-stamps; native kind (claude): `curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash`, self-update via `$REAL_BIN install`); pnpm launcher (failure-throttled); `yolo` wrapper → `uv run --no-project --with typer --with rich --with pyjson5 -- python ~/.yolo-shims/_yolo_bootstrap.py` (sys.path.insert repo_root; argv[0]='yolo'). generate_shims rmtree+recreates SHIM_DIR every boot; launchers skip names already shimmed (block order matters). Also deletes stale ~/.local/bin/yolo.
public_surface.consumers: every PATH lookup in jail; nested-jail yolo CLI

public_surface.name: agent config outputs
public_surface.detail: copilot: ~/.copilot/{config.json ('{"yolo": true}' if absent), mcp-config.json (full rewrite), lsp-config.json}. gemini: ~/.gemini/settings.json merge (approvalMode:yolo, enablePermanentToolApproval, enableAutoUpdate:false) + yolo-managed-mcp-servers.json sidecar (managed-set reconciliation; legacy migration heuristics). claude: ~/.claude/settings.json (3-way host merge w/ snapshot yolo-host-synced-settings.json; permissions allow=[]/deny=[]/defaultMode=acceptEdits/additionalDirectories=["/"]; skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt; autoUpdaterStatus=disabled; enabledPlugins per LSP; env.ENABLE_LSP_TOOL) + ~/.claude.json (mcpServers w/ managed sidecar; projects./workspace enableAllProjectMcpServers+hasTrustDialogAccepted). opencode: ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json ($schema, permission:"allow", mcp entries {type:local, command:argv-array, environment}) + sidecar. pi: ~/.pi/agent/settings.json defaultProjectTrust:"always". codex: ~/.codex/config.toml (approval_policy="never", sandbox_mode="danger-full-access", [mcp_servers.*]; custom TOML emitter drops non-mcp nested user tables with warning) + sidecar. All JSON written json.dumps(indent=2)+"\n", user keys preserved.
public_surface.consumers: the 6 agent CLIs; yolo check re-parses these

public_surface.name: claude credentials symlink + harvest
public_surface.detail: ~/.claude/.credentials.json must be symlink → ../.claude-shared-credentials/.credentials.json. A regular file found there (post-/login) is harvested into the shared file: metadata keys (scopes/subscriptionType/rateLimitTier) grafted if truthy; token trio (accessToken/refreshToken/expiresAt) adopted only if expiresAt strictly newer; atomic mkstemp+fchmod(0600)+os.replace. Coordinates with host oauth broker (src/oauth_broker.py) without cross-boundary locking.

public_surface.name: /run/yolo-services/cgroup-delegate.sock (client in generated yolo-cglimit)
public_surface.detail: Newline-delimited JSON request {op:"create_and_join", name, cpu_pct?, memory?, pids?} → response {ok, error?, warnings?[]}; then execvp of the command (daemon already moved caller via SO_PEERCRED). Entrypoint itself only checks socket existence and prints availability to stderr. Exit 1 on missing socket/error.

public_surface.name: journal bridge (generated yolo-journalctl)
public_surface.detail: Socket from YOLO_SERVICE_JOURNAL_SOCKET (default /run/yolo-services/journal.sock). Sends {"args":[...]}\n; reads frames: 5-byte header struct '>BI' (type 1=stdout, 2=stderr, 3=exit w/ 4-byte '>i' code); unknown types ignored. Exits with host code; 130 on KeyboardInterrupt; 1 + hint when socket absent. -h/--help prints own doc unless YOLO_JOURNALCTL_PASSTHROUGH_HELP=1.

public_surface.name: Boot-written files (parity-diff surface)
public_surface.detail: ~/.bashrc, ~/.yolo-bootstrap.sh, ~/.yolo-venv-precreate.sh, ~/.yolo-ca-bundle.crt, ~/.yolo-perf.log (append, trimmed to 50 runs), ~/.yolo-socat.log, ~/.config/mise/config.toml, ~/.config/nvim (copytree from /ctx/host-nvim-config, SameFile-tolerant), ~/.local/bin/{chrome-devtools-mcp-wrapper,yolo-cglimit,yolo-ps,yolo-journalctl}, ~/.local/bin/mcp-wrappers/{node,npx}, /run/{localtime,timezone,ld.so.cache}, /tmp/yolo-jail-supervisor.pid, ~/.claude/jail-history/<sha256[:12]>.jsonl (+history.jsonl symlink). MUTATES /workspace/mise.toml (strips retired agent tool lines). Many are single-file bind mounts pre-touched by cli (writes must be in-place, not rename!).

public_surface.name: YOLO_LSP_NPM_INSTALL / YOLO_LSP_GO_INSTALL (consumed by generated ~/.yolo-bootstrap.sh, not by python)
public_surface.detail: Newline-separated package lists; bootstrap installs missing, uninstalls entries present in ~/.yolo-installed-lsps sentinel ('kind:pkg' lines) but no longer desired; also installs chrome-devtools-mcp + sequential-thinking npm pkgs if missing, pip install showboat, fc-cache -f.
public_surface.consumers: cli setup_script runs bootstrap after mise install

public_surface.name: subprocess side-invocations
public_surface.detail: mise trust --all --quiet (cwd=/workspace); mise uninstall --all <retired> (timeout 30); git/jj config; claude plugins install|uninstall <id> (timeout 30, YOLO_BYPASS_SHIMS=1); ldconfig; socat; python -m src.jail_daemon_supervisor. All best-effort, output swallowed.

public_surface.name: shim exit codes
public_surface.detail: Blocked-tool shims exit 127; launchers exit 1 when binary unavailable after install attempt; yolo-cglimit/journalctl exit 1 on missing socket; entrypoint main itself never exits normally (execs bash).

internal_deps: src.yolo_ps (imported by generated yolo-ps script at runtime)
src.cli (imported by generated yolo wrapper via _yolo_bootstrap.py)

REVERSE deps — host CLI imports THIS package: src/cli/run_cmd.py (AGENTS, YOLO_FLAG_ALIASES, full dry-run of generate_*/CONFIG_WRITERS in `yolo check`), src/cli/config.py + storage.py + agents_md.py (agent_registry data), src/cli/macos_user.py (imports entrypoint, rebinds entrypoint.WORKSPACE, runs subset of generators on native macOS)

third_party_deps.pkg: (none — stdlib only, by hard design constraint)
third_party_deps.used_for: package must run from the bare Nix python313 before any pip install; imports: json, os, re, shutil, subprocess, sys, time, pathlib, hashlib, tempfile, stat, shlex, socket, tomllib, dataclasses, typing
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: Go stdlib covers all of it; TOML parse needs BurntSushi/toml or pelletier/go-toml (tomllib equivalent)

side_effects: Writes ~30 files/scripts under $HOME (see public_surface); rmtree+recreates ~/.yolo-shims every boot
Mutates /workspace/mise.toml in the user's live-mounted repo (retired-tool strip)
Mutates os.environ (PATH, 4 CA vars, hydrated user-env keys) — inherited by all spawned children
Spawns long-lived children of PID 1: socat forwarders, jail-daemon supervisor (kernel-reaped on PID 1 exit)
iptables NAT table mutation (PREROUTING DNAT, appended per exec — duplicate rules on re-exec are latent but harmless today)
Runs mise uninstall / mise trust / git config --global / jj config / claude plugins subprocesses
Terminal output: stderr status lines (cgroup delegate availability, warnings, '⚡ Executing: …' in cyan ANSI)
Final os.execvp replaces the process with bash (never returns)
python_specifics: Import-time module constants from env (HOME, SHIM_DIR, MISE_SHIMS…) + tests/host callers REBIND them after import (entrypoint.HOME = tmp_path, entrypoint.WORKSPACE = …); submodules deliberately use lazy `from . import HOME` inside functions to honor rebinding — Go needs an explicit config struct built at startup instead, and the host-side rebinding call sites break entirely

Host CLI imports this package as a LIBRARY (yolo check dry-run subprocess, macos_user bootstrap) and calls individual generate_*/configure_* — a Go binary can't be imported; port needs a step-selection CLI mode (e.g. `yolo-entrypoint --only shims,bashrc --home DIR`) or those callers stay on Python

Dict insertion-order preservation is observable: merged JSON configs keep user key order + append new keys; Go encoding/json sorts map keys — byte-level parity requires an ordered-map JSON encoder (or relaxed structural parity tests)
json.dumps(indent=2) exact formatting (space after colon, no trailing spaces) must be matched for golden-file diffs; same for the hand-rolled TOML emitters (_dump_codex_toml, mise config)
tomllib (read) + custom minimal TOML writers; mise.toml updated via MULTILINE regexes on raw text, not parse/serialize — port regexes as-is (RE2-safe, no backrefs)
Verbose regex with named groups (_EXPORT_RE) incl. '\'' unescaping — translate carefully
os.execvp final exec (syscall.Exec in Go — fine, but no libc PATH search: use exec.LookPath first)
os.kill(pid, 0) liveness probe distinguishing ProcessLookupError vs PermissionError (EPERM counts as alive) — Go: syscall.Kill(pid,0) errno check
subprocess.Popen with start_new_session=False (same process group as PID 1), close_fds, DEVNULL — Go default matches but be explicit
shutil.copytree(dirs_exist_ok, ignore_dangling_symlinks) + shutil.Error/SameFileError swallow for nested-jail same-inode — hand-roll in Go with inode comparison
tempfile.mkstemp + os.fchmod(0600) + os.replace atomic write pattern (credentials harvest)
Broad `except Exception` best-effort wrappers around every configure step — Go port must preserve 'never abort boot' semantics per step

Generated helper scripts ARE Python (yolo-cglimit, yolo-journalctl, yolo-ps, _yolo_bootstrap.py) and depend on in-image python3 + the mounted Python repo (src.yolo_ps, src.cli) — Go entrypoint can emit them byte-identically at first; replacing the scripts themselves is a separate later unit
int(entry) parsing of YOLO_FORWARD_HOST_PORTS raises uncaught ValueError on garbage string (crashes boot) — parity quirk to decide on, don't silently 'fix'
hashlib.sha256(...)[:12] history key; time.monotonic perf log format ('%7.3fs' alignment) if byte-parity of perf log matters (it shouldn't)

seams.name: /bin/yolo-entrypoint image wrapper (THE seam)
seams.contract: argv → same file tree under $HOME + /run + /tmp, same children spawned, same final exec of `bash --rcfile ~/.bashrc -c <wrapped cmd>`; full YOLO_*/TZ/CA env contract above; idempotent across repeated `podman exec cname yolo-entrypoint <cmd>`
seams.swap_mechanism: flake.nix's writeTextFile wrapper currently does `exec python3 -m entrypoint`. Ship a static Go binary in the image alongside the Python pkg and branch in the wrapper on an env var (e.g. YOLO_ENTRYPOINT_IMPL=go|python, forwarded by cli via -e) — both impls in one image enables per-jail A/B with zero host changes. NOTE: any change here requires image rebuild + `just load && just install` on the host; src/cli changes do not.
seams.parity_test: Boot two jails (impl=python vs impl=go) with identical config; diff the ws_state overlay trees + `podman exec` env; run tests/test_entrypoint.py-derived golden fixtures. The `yolo check` dry-run harness (run_cmd.py ~l.900) is already exactly this: run generators into a tmp HOME with a synthetic env and parse outputs — point it at both impls and diff

seams.name: yolo check dry-run + macos_user bootstrap (host-side library import)
seams.contract: Host CLI runs generate_shims/agent_launchers/bashrc/bootstrap/venv_precreate/mise_config/mcp_wrappers + selected CONFIG_WRITERS against a temp/sandbox HOME, then re-parses the JSON/TOML outputs
seams.swap_mechanism: Replace the inline `python -c` code block with `yolo-entrypoint-go --dry-run --home $TMP --steps …` subprocess; env contract is unchanged (JAIL_HOME etc. already passed via env). macos_user needs an equivalent --native mode (skips Linux-only steps, overrides WORKSPACE)
seams.parity_test: Run both against same tmp HOME+env, diff output trees byte-for-byte; existing test_cli_check_formatting / test_macos_user tests

seams.name: agent_registry data (imported by 4 host CLI modules)
seams.contract: AGENTS map (install kind/bin/package/flags/installer_url, config_writer name, briefing paths, overlay_dirs, skills, yolo_flags, alias, mise_retire), DEFAULT_AGENTS, YOLO_FLAG_ALIASES, ALL_MISE_RETIRE, ALL_OVERLAY_DIRS
seams.swap_mechanism: Pure data — extract to a checked-in JSON/embedded spec consumed by both Python (until cli ports) and Go; or port last, after cli. Keep the Python module as generated-from-JSON during transition
seams.parity_test: Dump registry to JSON from both impls, diff

seams.name: generated helper scripts (yolo-cglimit, yolo-journalctl, yolo-ps, yolo wrapper, bootstrap/venv/bashrc, shims, launchers)
seams.contract: Exact script text (they run out-of-process; agents/tests grep their behavior); socket protocols they speak (cgroup JSON-line, journal '>BI' frames)
seams.swap_mechanism: Go emits identical byte strings (embed via //go:embed) — zero behavioral risk. Later, individually swap script bodies for small Go binaries (each is its own process boundary; e.g. a Go yolo-cglimit only has to honor the JSON-line protocol)
seams.parity_test: sha256 of every generated file, python vs go

seams.name: jail-daemon supervisor spawn
seams.contract: Spawn `<python> -m src.jail_daemon_supervisor` w/ PYTHONPATH=$YOLO_REPO_ROOT, DEVNULL stdio, same pgroup, write PID to /tmp/yolo-jail-supervisor.pid; singleton via PID-file liveness
seams.swap_mechanism: Go entrypoint keeps exec'ing the Python supervisor unchanged (process boundary); supervisor ports independently later
seams.parity_test: boot, repeated podman exec, assert exactly one supervisor and broker port bound

seams.name: container-side socat forwarding
seams.contract: socat TCP-LISTEN:p,bind=127.0.0.1,fork,reuseaddr → UNIX-CONNECT:/tmp/yolo-fwd/port-p.sock | TCP:gateway:p; host half in cli/network.py untouched
seams.swap_mechanism: Go spawns identical socat argv (or later replaces socat with native Go proxy — separate unit)
seams.parity_test: curl through forwarded port in both impls; test class TestContainerPortForwarding

existing: tests/test_entrypoint.py — 197 tests / 31 classes covering essentially every function: shim generation (argv-filter matrix), bashrc, CA bundle, ld cache, timezone, all 6 agent configs (incl. 3-way claude settings merge, credentials harvest/symlink, managed-MCP reconciliation, codex TOML round-trip), mcp wrappers, mise config update/dedupe/retire, bootstrap + venv-precreate script content, agent/package-manager launchers, port forwarding, _port_in_use, perf logging, exec_bash, cglimit script, cgroup delegation, main() sequencing, supervisor singleton, user-env hydration
tests/test_cli_unit.py — host `import entrypoint` without MISE_DATA_DIR must not crash
tests/test_macos_user.py — JAIL_HOME-set-before-import ordering, native bootstrap subset
`yolo check` itself is a runtime smoke of all writers + output parse
gaps: No golden-file byte-level fixtures — tests assert structurally (json.loads), so a Go port with different key order/whitespace would pass Python tests but produce different files; add sha256 goldens before porting
No real-container end-to-end test in CI (repo docs mandate manual nested-jail verification: `yolo -- bash` from inside a jail); mount/EROFS/single-file-bind-mount write semantics only surface there
iptables DNAT and socat behavior only unit-mocked; duplicate-DNAT-rule accumulation on re-exec untested
Generated Python helper scripts' runtime behavior (yolo-cglimit/journalctl socket protocol) tested lightly vs their daemons
Concurrent-boot races (shared-HOME rmtree of SHIM_DIR, credentials harvest race) not exercised
test_coverage.assessment: Unusually strong unit coverage for a bootstrap module — the porting risk is not logic regressions but byte-format and mount-semantics parity, which current tests don't pin.

porting_hazards: Single-file bind mounts (.bashrc? no — but yolo-bootstrap.sh, yolo-venv-precreate.sh, yolo-perf.log, yolo-socat.log, yolo-ca-bundle.crt, yolo-installed-lsps, yolo-user-env.sh, yolo-entrypoint.lock are pre-touched host files mounted 1:1): writes MUST be in-place truncate+write, never tmp+rename (EBUSY/EXDEV on bind mounts) — Python's write_text is in-place; a Go 'atomic write' habit would break boot. The credentials fix history in agent_configs is the cautionary tale

JSON key ordering: Python preserves insertion order when merging user configs; Go map+encoding/json sorts keys → every merged config file (gemini settings.json, claude settings.json/.claude.json, opencode.json) would be rewritten with reordered user keys. Need ordered-JSON (e.g. custom decoder keeping order) for true no-functional-change parity

Dual-role package: same code is container PID-1 bootstrap AND host-importable library (yolo check, macos_user, agent_registry consumers). Go can replace the container role first; host callers need either a subprocess CLI shim or must port together — plan the dependency order

Image rebuild required for every iteration (flake.nix bakes the package; `just load && just install`), and Go binary must be cross-built for the Linux image on macOS hosts too (flake builds the farm on darwin — same constraint that forced generate_ld_cache to runtime)
exec_bash PATH string, bashrc content, and launcher bodies are consumed by agents and greppable docs — treat all generated text as frozen byte contracts (embed, don't re-template)
MISE_SHIMS fallback logic and JAIL_HOME-vs-HOME precedence are import-time in Python; Go must evaluate at process start with identical precedence (JAIL_HOME > HOME > /home/agent)

_hydrate_env_from_user_env_file must stay in lockstep with the writer format in cli/run_cmd.py (export KEY=${KEY:-'val'}, '\'' escaping) — cross-language now means the sync comment spans repos/languages

Idempotency under repeated `podman exec … yolo-entrypoint`: every step re-runs on each exec (shims rmtree'd, configs re-merged, DNAT re-appended, ports skipped-if-bound, supervisor PID-file-guarded) — subtle invariants, verify each in the nested-jail live test, not just unit tests
Three-way settings merge (_sync_settings_level) is one-level-deep-only by design with asymmetric deep flag on adds vs rollbacks — easy to 'improve' accidentally in a rewrite; port mechanically
Best-effort philosophy: nearly every failure path is swallowed (warn + continue) — Go's explicit error handling invites accidental fail-fast; boot must never abort
mise.toml regex surgery targets quoted tokens like '"npm:@github/copilot"' verbatim (token includes the quotes) — the re.escape of a quoted token is deliberate; replicate exactly
Mutating /workspace/mise.toml touches the user's live host checkout — a Go bug here corrupts user files, highest-blast-risk single write in the module
notes: Boot order in main() is load-bearing: hydrate user-env → timezone → ld cache → shims → agent launchers → pkg-mgr launchers → CA bundle (before bashrc, and before any child spawn so os.environ exports propagate) → bashrc → bootstrap/venv scripts → mise config → nvim copy → mcp wrappers → git/jj → per-agent writers (YOLO_AGENTS order) → cgroup check → helper scripts → DNAT → socat → supervisor → PATH set → mise trust → perf dump → exec bash. Deliberate non-call of `mise hook-env` (flock deadlock) is documented in three places — preserve. Recommended port sequencing: (1) freeze byte goldens from Python, (2) Go binary behind YOLO_ENTRYPOINT_IMPL in the flake wrapper, (3) leave generated Python helper scripts + supervisor spawn as-is, (4) convert yolo-check/macos_user callers to the subprocess dry-run mode, (5) port helper scripts individually against their socket protocols. All file paths absolute under /workspace/src/entrypoint/; primary consumers: /workspace/flake.nix (l.468-528), /workspace/src/cli/run_cmd.py, /workspace/src/cli/macos_user.py, /workspace/tests/test_entrypoint.py.
